narration: Ring species are a fascinating example of evolution, connecting populations around the globe.

narration: Imagine a species that can breed with its neighbors, but not with those at the ends of the circle.

narration: One classic example is the greenish warbler. Two populations, separated by thousands of miles, show gradual changes.

narration: This challenges our ideas of species and evolution. It's not always black and white.
